archetype: ARCHETYPE-3 (Adapter package) for `@netscript/auth-kv-oauth` — a self-hosted, no-DB OAuth2/OIDC Relying-Party adapter that CONSUMES the merged #77 `AuthenticatorPort`/`AuthnRequest`/`AuthnResult`/`Principal` from `@netscript/service/auth` (no new public port) and persists sessions in the framework's own Deno KV via `@netscript/kv` `WatchableKv`. Sibling of the Track-2 `@netscript/auth-workos` / `@netscript/auth-better-auth` adapters; completes the tier matrix (enterprise-SaaS / self-host+SQL / self-host+KV-no-DB). Protocol mechanics are delegated to a certified engine, not hand-rolled. Precedent: `packages/prisma-adapter-mysql/` (flat standalone adapter package) + the Track-2 auth adapters (dual surface: Hono mount helper + AuthenticatorPort factory).
scopeOverlays: SCOPE-service (targets the @netscript/service composition root: withAuthn({ authenticator }) injection + Hono mount helper owning /signin /callback /signout + refresh-on-read Set-Cookie emission through AuthnResult.setCookies)
lockedDecisions: Protocol engine = jsr:@panva/oauth4webapi@^3.8.6 (inline JSR specifier; verified 2026-06-20, JSR latest 3.8.6 == npm 3.8.6). Zero-dep, Web-Platform-only (fetch + Web Crypto), Deno-supported, MIT, OpenID-certified Basic/FAPI1/FAPI2 RP. NO hand-rolled OAuth/crypto. jose is NOT a baseline dep (id_token validation is internal to oauth4webapi); jsr:@panva/jose@^6.2.3 deferred to a later need to mint/verify custom JWTs.

Ports stay upstream: CONSUME AuthenticatorPort/AuthnRequest/AuthnResult/Principal from @netscript/service/auth (merged #77); never redefine. No @netscript/service public-surface change. Principal.scheme:'custom' + provider/session metadata in Principal.claims.

KV access = @netscript/kv WatchableKv (injectable, default await getKv()); typed key tuples; atomic() versionstamp CAS for refresh rotation. NEVER raw Deno.openKv in adapter code. Two namespaces: ['auth-kv-oauth','txn',id] (state/nonce?/codeVerifier/successUrl/issuer, 10-min TTL, single-use atomic check+delete) and ['auth-kv-oauth','session',id] (principalSnapshot/enc(tokens)/expiresAt/refreshMeta, default 90d, CAS-rotated, deleted on signOut).

Dual surface: mountKvOAuthHandler(app,{flow}) (Hono, owns /signin /callback /signout) + createKvOAuthAuthenticator(options): AuthenticatorPort (per-request), plus a lower-level createKvOAuthFlow(options). Mirrors the Track-2 better-auth/WorkOS adapters; WorkOS adapter is the closest analog for cookie-options + refresh-mode + setCookies emission (persist to KV instead of a sealed cookie).

Provider model = ONE generic builder defineOAuthProvider + a data registry of presets. v1 first-class (static endpoints): GitHub, Google, GitLab, Discord, Slack, Spotify, Facebook, Twitter/X. Tier-2 (issuer/domain-templated, OIDC/enterprise): Auth0, Okta, AWS Cognito, Azure AD, Logto, Clerk. Generic-only documented recipes (not maintained presets): Azure ADB2C, Notion, Dropbox, Patreon. OIDC presets prefer discovery (issuer); endpoints overridable; NO version-pinned literals (Facebook v17.0 / pre-X Twitter domains are stale in the reference — verify at impl).

Security baseline (must certify): PKCE S256 always (never plain); exact state; RFC 9207 iss mix-up defense; OIDC nonce + id_token validation; AES-256-GCM token-at-rest sealing; refresh-token rotation + reuse detection; __Host-/HttpOnly/Secure/SameSite=Lax cookies; isHttps derived from forwarded-proto/config (not raw URL, which breaks behind TLS-terminating proxies); open-redirect allowlist on returnTo/successUrl (the reference trusts the success_url query param — a real hole); HTTPS enforced (no allowInsecureRequests outside dev tests).

Token-encryption key provenance (LOCKED): AES-256-GCM key supplied to createKvOAuthStore as an injected CryptoKey/ArrayBuffer OR resolved from required env secret NETSCRIPT_AUTH_KV_OAUTH_KEY (32-byte base64url). Sealed blobs carry a keyId prefix so future rotation is non-breaking; active key-rotation deferred to debt. Missing key => hard startup error, never a silent plaintext fallback.

RP-only for v1 (LOCKED): adapter is an OAuth/OIDC Relying-Party client only. Resource-server bearer-JWT validation (validateJwtAccessToken) is OUT OF SCOPE / deferred (that remains the existing seam authenticators' job). Public PKCE-only clients (clientAuthMethod:'none') ARE supported.

Catalog law: engine pinned via inline jsr: (no package.json/catalog entry at baseline) — intentional divergence from Track-2 adapters routing npm libs (jose/better-auth/@workos-inc/node) through catalog:. Confirm acceptability at PLAN-EVAL. Confirm 'latest' via deno task deps:latest at impl, never deno outdated --latest. deno.json catalog, aspire/src/public/mod.ts, scaffold-versions.ts, version pins UNTOUCHED (LD-8).

NOT an e2e-cli-gate slice: standalone additive adapter package, no @netscript/cli touch, no scaffold-output/Aspire-wiring change -> do NOT attach e2e-cli-gate label or run scaffold.runtime smoke. arch:check baseline unchanged (new adapter follows ARCHETYPE-3 cardinality).

Attribution: MIT notice crediting the Deno authors ('Copyright 2023-2024 the Deno authors. MIT license.') for the provider endpoint catalog + getRequiredEnv pattern REIMPLEMENTED (not wrapped) from deno_kv_oauth. The stale @cmd-johnson/oauth2-client is NOT a dependency.
contracts: AuthenticatorPort / AuthnRequest (header/headers()/cookie()/method/path) / AuthnResult ({ ok, principal, responseHeaders?, setCookies? } | { ok:false, reason }) / Principal (subject/scopes/roles/scheme/claims) — REUSED unchanged from @netscript/service/auth; this adapter is a new external implementor (scheme:'custom').

OAuthProviderConfig (clientId/clientSecret?/issuer?/authorizationEndpoint?/tokenEndpoint?/userInfoEndpoint?/redirectUri/scopes/clientAuthMethod?/pkce?/par?/extraAuthParams?) + defineOAuthProvider(input): OAuthProviderConfig + providers preset registry (first-class + tier-2). New package surface.

KvOAuthStore over WatchableKv: createKvOAuthStore({ kv?, namespace?, sessionTtlMs?, txnTtlMs?, encryptionKey?, keyId? }) -> { putTxn/takeTxn(single-use atomic)/putSession/getSession/rotateSession(CAS)/deleteSession }. New package surface.

createKvOAuthFlow(options): { signIn/handleCallback/getSessionId/signOut } + createKvOAuthAuthenticator(options): AuthenticatorPort (cookie -> KV session -> near-expiry refresh via refreshTokenGrantRequest -> CAS rotate -> Principal + setCookies) + mountKvOAuthHandler(app,{basePath?,flow}): registers GET signin, GET callback, POST signout. New package surface.
Engine: jsr:@panva/oauth4webapi@^3.8.6 inline specifier (NOT a catalog entry); jose deferred. KvOAuthError taxonomy with KvOAuthErrorCode union mapping 1:1 to AuthnResult.reason strings.
testPlan: unit store: KV txn round-trip + single-use atomic check+delete; session put/get; CAS rotateSession (versionstamp); deleteSession. Injected WatchableKv (in-memory/test KV).
unit crypto: AES-256-GCM seal/unseal round-trip; keyId prefix present; missing-key hard error; tamper -> decrypt failure.
unit cookies: __Host- attribute set; HttpOnly/Secure/SameSite=Lax; isHttps derived from x-forwarded-proto (proxy case) not raw URL; parse round-trip.

flow against a stubbed Authorization Server: signIn mints state+nonce+PKCE and 302s to authorize URL; handleCallback success path (validateAuthResponse + code grant + id_token validation); state mismatch rejected; nonce mismatch rejected; AS error param surfaced; expired/absent txn rejected; open-redirect returnTo rejected by allowlist.

authenticator: cookie -> KV session -> Principal mapping (default id_token claims; optional userinfo); session-missing reason string; near-expiry refresh -> CAS rotate -> setCookies emitted; refresh-token reuse detected -> session revoked.
integration (Hono): mountKvOAuthHandler full signin -> callback -> authenticate -> signout on a Hono app; auth routes exempt from the authenticator.
JSR readiness: deno check --unstable-kv ./mod.ts; publish:dry-run; doc-lint over the FULL export map (not mod.ts alone).
planGateSelfCheck: Research present and current: PASS — 5-agent reverse-engineering workflow (wf_65d060d7) file-cited against local clones of deno_kv_oauth / @cmd-johnson/oauth2-client / oauth4webapi; integration surface re-verified against the MERGED #77 seam (packages/service/src/auth) and @netscript/kv WatchableKv on main; engine coordinates verified live (JSR+npm both 3.8.6). Decisions locked: PASS — 11 locked decisions with rationale (engine, ports-upstream, KV/WatchableKv, dual surface, provider model, security baseline, encryption-key provenance, RP-only, catalog/JSR-inline, non-e2e-gate, attribution). Open-decision sweep: PASS — encryption-key provenance and RP-only now LOCKED; the one remaining open item (JSR-inline engine pin vs Track-2 catalog: convention) is a cross-cutting policy call surfaced explicitly for PLAN-EVAL, not a rework-forcing decision. Commit slices: PASS — 6 ordered, dependency-gated slices (S1 scaffold+providers -> S2 store+crypto+cookies -> S3 flow -> S4 authenticator+refresh -> S5 mount+integration+README -> S6 gates+debt), each names files + gate; 6 < 30. Risk register: PASS — RP maintenance burden (bounded to certified-engine auth-code+PKCE+OIDC path), provider-literal drift (verify at impl / prefer discovery), JSR-vs-catalog divergence. Gate set: PASS — ARCHETYPE-3 + SCOPE-service; scoped run-deno-check/-lint/-fmt wrappers, per-package deno test --unstable-kv, deno check --unstable-kv ./mod.ts, publish:dry-run, JSR doc-lint full-export, arch:check, deps:latest/audit; e2e:cli explicitly EXCLUDED with rationale. jsr-audit: PASS — applied to the new package surface (full export map, not mod.ts alone). Residual FAIL_PLAN risk: LOW-MEDIUM — most likely challenge is the JSR-inline-vs-catalog engine-pin policy and whether v1 provider scope (8 first-class + 6 tier-2) is too large for one lane; plan offers tier-2/generic-recipe split + PAR/DPoP deferral as acceptable rescope.
openQuestions: RESOLVED (locked above): encryption-key provenance (env NETSCRIPT_AUTH_KV_OAUTH_KEY or injected key + keyId prefix; rotation deferred) and RP-only v1 (resource-server validateJwtAccessToken deferred).

POLICY CALL for PLAN-EVAL: JSR-inline engine pin (jsr:@panva/oauth4webapi inline, no package.json/catalog entry) vs routing the engine through the workspace catalog: for consistency with auth-workos/auth-better-auth (which catalog npm libs). Plan keeps inline jsr: as doctrine-correct for a Deno-native JSR pkg; flags divergence for ruling.

RESCOPE-CANDIDATE for PLAN-EVAL: v1 provider scope (8 first-class + 6 tier-2 presets) may be large for one lane; tier-2 presets and/or PAR+DPoP opt-ins can split to a fast-follow leaving first-class presets + core RP flow in this lane. Plan keeps them together but flags the split as acceptable.

VERIFY-ITEM (not a decision): provider endpoint literals (X.com, Facebook API version) are stale in the reference — re-verify at impl; prefer OIDC discovery where available. Token-encryption key wiring in the NetScript runtime (env secret vs Aspire-provisioned) is an impl-time integration detail, not a plan blocker.
risks: Taking on maintenance of an OAuth Relying-Party client; mitigated by delegating all security-critical mechanics to the OpenID-certified oauth4webapi engine, bounding scope to the well-trodden auth-code+PKCE+OIDC path, and a conformance-style test suite covering state/nonce/iss/open-redirect/refresh-reuse.

Provider-literal drift (X.com domains, Facebook API version pinned v17.0 in the stale reference); mitigated by preferring OIDC discovery for issuer-based providers, making endpoint fields overridable, and a verify-at-impl gate — no version-pinned literals.

JSR-inline engine pin diverges from Track-2's catalog: convention; mitigated by surfacing it as an explicit PLAN-EVAL policy call with a documented doctrine rationale (npm->catalog:, JSR->inline jsr:).

Scope size: 8 first-class + 6 tier-2 provider presets plus full RP flow + KV store + crypto + mount across one lane is large; mitigated by 6 commit-by-slice gates and a documented tier-2 / PAR / DPoP split as an acceptable PLAN-EVAL rescope.
